home.hero_subtitle: We are committed to protecting your data. Explore our privacy philosophies across all project ecosystems.
home.philosophy_text: Privacy is not a feature, it's a fundamental right. In every project, from browser extensions to web apps, we always adhere to the principle: Your data belongs to you.
home.security_text: End-to-end encryption and local storage are top priorities.
home.transparency_text: No hidden data collection, no selling of user information.

projects.title: PROJECT LIST
projects.subtitle: Every tool is a promise of safety.

bookmark_manager.name: Privacy Bookmark
bookmark_manager.desc: The sole purpose of this extension is to provide users with an easy interface to manage bookmarks (view, organize, search, and edit).

startpage.name: Zero Startpage - Newtab Replacement
startpage.desc: Replaces Chrome new tab with a customizable productivity dashboard while keeping user data local-first.

coming_soon.name: Coming Soon
coming_soon.desc: New projects are being developed with the same privacy-first philosophy.

privacy.title: Privacy Policy
privacy.internal_desc: All bookmark operations are performed locally in your browser. No personal data is collected.
privacy.bookmarks_desc: This permission is required to access the user's bookmarks in order to display them, organize folders, search by title or URL, edit bookmark details, and detect and remove duplicate bookmarks automatically.
privacy.storage_desc: The storage permission is used to save user preferences, such as the preferred quick-open behavior (popup, side panel, or web view), so the extension can remember and apply these settings across sessions.
privacy.sidePanel_desc: The sidePanel permission is required to open the bookmark manager interface inside Chrome’s side panel, allowing users to view and manage their bookmarks without leaving or interrupting the current browsing page.
privacy.tabs_desc: The tabs permission is used to detect whether the bookmark manager page is already open, activate and focus an existing tab if found, or create a new tab when necessary to prevent duplicate pages and improve user experience.
privacy.systemDisplay_desc: The system.display permission is used to obtain screen and work area dimensions in order to correctly position the extension’s popup window on the user’s screen, ensuring that the popup appears fully visible and accessible across different screen sizes and resolutions.
privacy.webNavigation_desc: The webNavigation permission is required to track user interactions with bookmarks. It allows the extension to monitor when users navigate to bookmarked URLs, both inside and outside the extension, ensuring accurate tracking and management of bookmark usage. This permission is essential for the core functionality of the extension.
privacy.chatbot_desc: The chatbot is now a command-based system and does not utilize AI or third-party APIs. This ensures complete privacy and local processing.
privacy.usage_general: Zero Bookmark Manager does not collect, sell, or transfer user data to third parties.
privacy.usage_bookmarks: Bookmark data is accessed only for the purpose of displaying, organizing, searching, editing, and removing duplicate bookmarks. All bookmark-related processing is performed locally within the user’s browser and is not transmitted to external servers.
privacy.usage_storage: User preferences and extension settings are stored locally using Chrome’s storage API and remain on the user’s device.
privacy.usage_ai: The extension includes an optional chatbot feature that allows users to connect their own selected AI provider (such as Google Gemini or OpenAI GPT). Only the text explicitly entered by the user into the chatbot is sent directly to the selected provider’s API to generate responses. No bookmark data, browsing history, or personal information is sent to external services by the extension.
privacy.usage_ai_policy: The extension does not log, retain, or analyze chatbot interactions. Any data sent to third-party AI providers is governed by the respective provider’s privacy policy and terms of service.
privacy.source_desc: The extension is open-source. You can review the code and releases on GitHub.
privacy.issues_desc: Minor UI refresh issues after moving items. Fix: Refresh page (F5).
privacy.contact_desc: If you have any questions or concerns, please contact at:

startpage_privacy.title: Zero Startpage - Newtab Replacement
startpage_privacy.subtitle: Privacy
startpage_privacy.intro: To facilitate compliance with Chrome Web Store Developer Program Policies, the following declarations describe the extension's single purpose and permission usage.
startpage_privacy.single_purpose_desc: Startpage replaces Chrome's new tab page with a customizable personal dashboard. It provides local bookmark management, clock/calendar widgets, search shortcuts, optional background and visual effects, and optional media controls/visualization for active audio tabs. Its single purpose is to give users a personalized and productivity-focused new tab experience.
startpage_privacy.bookmarks_desc: The bookmarks permission is used only when the user chooses to import bookmarks into Startpage. The extension reads the bookmark tree via Chrome Bookmarks API so users can select and import items into their local Startpage view. No bookmark data is sold, shared for unrelated purposes, or sent to our own servers.
startpage_privacy.scripting_desc: The scripting permission is used to execute short in-page scripts in the currently detected media tab to read playback metadata (title, artist, duration, paused state) and to perform user-requested media controls (play/pause/next/previous/seek). It is also used for optional audio visualizer synchronization. Scripts are packaged with the extension and are not remotely loaded.
startpage_privacy.tabs_desc: The tabs permission is used to locate audible/media tabs and read tab URL/title context needed to identify supported media pages (for example YouTube/Spotify) and show current playback information in the Startpage widget. It is not used for advertising, profiling, or sale of browsing data.
startpage_privacy.host_intro: Host permissions are required for three extension features:
startpage_privacy.host_item1: Requesting search suggestions from suggestqueries.google.com when the user types in the search box.
startpage_privacy.host_item2: Reading media state and controlling playback on supported media sites (such as YouTube and Spotify) via injected scripts.
startpage_privacy.host_item3: Supporting optional media detection broadly across pages due to the extension's cross-site media widget behavior.
startpage_privacy.host_outro: All access is feature-scoped and initiated by extension functionality; no remote code execution is used.
startpage_privacy.source_desc: The extension source is public for transparency. You can review implementation details and release history on GitHub.
startpage_privacy.contact_desc: For compliance or privacy questions, contact:
startpage_privacy.footer: Accurate and transparent declarations help reduce review time and rejection risk.
